records.ety: [L. abyssus a bottomless gulf, fr. Gr. <?/ bottomless; 'a priv. + <?/ depth, bottom.]

defs: A bottomless or unfathomed depth, gulf, or chasm; hence, any deep, immeasurable, and, specifically, hell, or the bottomless pit.
quotes: Ye powers and spirits of this nethermost abyss.
The throne is darkness, in the abyss of light.

defs: Infinite time; a vast intellectual or moral depth.

notes: ☞ This word, in its leading uses, is associated with the cosmological notions of the Hebrews, having reference to a supposed illimitable mass of waters from which our earth sprung, and beneath whose profound depths the wicked were punished.
